sections.paragraph:
μυσερός, ά, όν (μύσος ‘defilement’; as μυσαρός Eur., Hdt. et al.; Ins. aus d, Asklepieion v. Kos A, 22 [III b.c.]: RHerzog, ARW 10, 1907, 402; SibOr 3, 500. On the spelling s. B-D-F §29, 1. In LXX only Lev 18:23, where μυσερός is better attested; s. Thackeray 75.—PGM 2, 148 ἁγνὸς ἀπὸ παντὸς μυσεροῦ; Manetho, Apotel. 4, 269 μυσερός is v.l.; Etym. Mag. 535, 32; 566, 43 μυσε. beside μυσα.; Malalas [VI a.d.] μυσε. [Psaltes p. 2]) loathsome, abominable, detestable ζῆλος 1 Cl 14:1. μοιχεία 30:1.—DELG s.v. μύσος.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

ARW ARW = Archiv für Religionswissenschaft—List 6

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Manetho Manetho , hist., III b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

Malalas Malalas = John Malalas, Chronographia VI a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 661
